name: Manzanedo Valley and Ebro River Loop
name_original: Cuevas de San Pedro por el Valle de Manzanedo - Río Ebro — circular desde Quintana de Valdivielso
description: This difficult 38.7-mile road cycling route from Quintana de Valdivielso offers challenging climbs and scenic views in the Ebro Valley.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Merindad De Valdivielso traverse a landscape shaped by the Ebro River, which carves deep canyons and gorges through the territory. The region is framed by the steep cliffs of the Sierra de La Tesla to the north and the calcareous páramos of La Lora to the south, offering varied terrain. Cyclists will encounter lush river valleys, extensive fruit orchards, and diverse natural vegetation including holm oaks and pines. Geological formations like the Tesla-Canales Anticline contribute to the dramatic and varied topography. Best road cycling routes around Merindad De Valdivielso The most popular road cycling route is Cueva de los Moros y Torre de los Verona — circular por la Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche, a 58.2 miles (93.8 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete, passing historical towers and natural caves. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cuevas de San Pedro por el Valle de Manzanedo - Río Ebro — circular desde Quintana de Valdivielso, a difficult 38.7 miles (62.2 km) path that explores river valleys and historical cave systems. Local road cyclists also love the Frías Bridge – MonasterioDeSanSalvador loop from Trespaderne, a 61.4 miles (98.9 km) trail leading through historical landmarks, often completed in about 4 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around Merindad De Valdivielso is defined by the Ebro River valley, the cliffs of the Sierra de La Tesla, and charming villages with Romanesque churches. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Merindad De Valdivielso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 1800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Merindad De Valdivielso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: Road cycling in Merindad De Valdivielso offers a diverse landscape. You'll encounter routes shaped by the Ebro River, carving deep canyons and gorges. The region is framed by the steep cliffs of the Sierra de La Tesla to the north and the calcareous páramos of La Lora to the south, providing varied terrain from river valleys to more elevated sections. Expect a mix of climbs and descents, with some routes featuring significant elevation gains.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available in Merindad De Valdivielso, catering to various skill levels. This includes a good mix of moderate and difficult options, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Merindad De Valdivielso offers several easier road cycling routes. While many routes feature challenging terrain, there are at least 6 routes classified as easy, allowing for a more relaxed exploration of the region's beauty.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenges. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058233044/cuevas-de-san-pedro-por-el-valle-de-manzanedo-rio-ebro-circular-desde-quintana-de-valdivielso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de San Pedro por el Valle de Manzanedo - Río Ebro — circular desde Quintana de Valdivielso&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 62.2 km path exploring river valleys and historical cave systems. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías Bridge – MonasterioDeSanSalvador loop from Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt;, a 98.9 km trail with over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the region?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Merindad De Valdivielso are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1060483565/cueva-de-los-moros-y-torre-de-los-verona-circular-por-la-sierra-de-aracena-y-picos-de-aroche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Moros y Torre de los Verona — circular por la Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058233044/cuevas-de-san-pedro-por-el-valle-de-manzanedo-rio-ebro-circular-desde-quintana-de-valdivielso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de San Pedro por el Valle de Manzanedo - Río Ebro — circular desde Quintana de Valdivielso&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while road cycling in Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You'll cycle alongside the Ebro River, through deep canyons and gorges like Desfiladero de los Hocinos. The landscape features extensive fruit orchards, diverse forests, and geological formations such as the Tesla-Canales Anticline. Panoramic views are a highlight, especially from areas like the Puerto de La Mazorra. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal Salt Flats&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. Merindad De Valdivielso is dotted with charming villages and historical sites. You can explore places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6318262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Center&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6318256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarcayo&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features Romanesque churches, stately mansions, and defensive towers. Notable cave systems like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5775442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de los Moros&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4977826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Penedo de los Portugueses Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; are also points of interest.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: The region's climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with fruit orchards in bloom or displaying rich fall colors. Summer can be warm, but routes along the Ebro River or through shaded forests provide relief. Winter cycling is possible, but conditions may vary, especially in higher elevations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Ebro River valley, and the opportunity to discover charming villages and historical sites along the way. The combination of challenging rides and picturesque landscapes is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, there is at least one route that includes a waterfall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón Waterfall – Alcázar of the Constables loop from Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 59.6 km route that allows you to experience this natural feature.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes that pass through Merindad De Valdivielso?
A: While specific road cycling routes are detailed, the region is part of longer national trails. The GR-85 "Ruta de los Sentidos" (165 km) and the GR-99 "Camino Natural del Ebro" (930 km) traverse the area, suggesting opportunities for integrating local routes into multi-day cycling adventures along well-conditioned paths that are suitable for road and gravel biking.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay along the road cycling routes?
A: The charming small villages integrated into the landscape, such as Valdenoceda, Quintana de Valdivielso, and Quecedo, often feature local amenities. While specific cafes or accommodations are not listed in the route data, these villages are good places to look for refreshments and lodging, especially in the municipal capital of Quecedo.
tips: Near the Argés road junction, a narrow path ascends to a beautiful hermitage, which, in addition to being used as a place of worship and housing, was used as a cemetery. Indeed, the Cueva de San Pedro is excavated in the rock on a mound on the outskirts of town.

The Villanueva-Rampalay bridge over the Ebro is a fantastic 13th century construction originally Gothic in style. In later centuries, modifications and interventions have been made that represent a variation of its original style.
Mountain pass known in the area as it is an area where the Vuelta España has passed a few times. It represents a second category port, which describes that it is already hard enough!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route is approximately 62.2 kilometers long. Given its 'difficult' grading and 924 meters of elevation gain, it typically takes around 3 hours and 3 minutes to complete for an experienced cyclist. However, your actual duration may vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' due to its significant elevation gain of 924 meters over 62.2 kilometers. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenging ride with considerable climbing.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, as indicated by 'circular desde Quintana de Valdivielso' in its name. You will start and finish in the same location, Quintana de Valdivielso.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available in Quintana de Valdivielso?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Quintana de Valdivielso. While specific dedicated cycling parking isn't widely documented, you can generally find public parking within the village. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most appropriate spots.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain in the Burgos province, particularly around the Ebro River and Valle de Manzanedo, is diverse. Expect a mix of roads that traverse mountains, valleys, and river sections. Given the 'difficult' grading and significant elevation, you will encounter substantial climbs, including the Ascent to the La Eme pass, and rewarding descents. The roads are likely paved, but conditions can vary on lesser-known local routes.

faqs.question: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the historic Hermitage of San Pedro, which is associated with cave complexes, the scenic Villanueva-Rampalay Bridge, and the challenging Ascent to the La Eme pass, offering panoramic views.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Camino natural de Ebro - Castilla y León (28%) and BCB Tres ríos: Por el Valle del Manzanedo, Ebro, Trifón y Nela (22%). You'll also find smaller portions overlapping with [CIMA BU04] La Mazorra, [CIMA BU05] La Eme, and the Ruta de Carlos V, among others.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Burgos province experiences diverse weather. Generally, late spring (May-June) and early autumn (September-October) offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cold with potential for snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, dogs are generally permitted if they are safely secured (e.g., in a bike trailer) and do not interfere with traffic or other users. However, if you plan to stop and explore any natural areas or villages, always keep your dog on a leash and adhere to local regulations, especially as parts of the route pass through the Parque Natural Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this route, especially within the Parque Natural Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads within the Parque Natural Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón. However, always respect natural park regulations, stay on designated paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife. If you plan to visit specific attractions or private properties along the way, check their individual access requirements.

faqs.question: What amenities are available near the starting point in Quintana de Valdivielso?
faqs.answer: Quintana de Valdivielso is a small village. While specific details for cyclists might be limited, you can typically expect basic amenities such as a local bar or restaurant, and potentially some small guesthouses or rural accommodation. For more extensive services like bike shops or larger supermarkets, you might need to visit a larger town in the region.
